# Transcrição: The FAATT Framework Summary & Recap

- **Fonte:** The FAATT Framework Summary & Recap
- **Duração:** 05:02

## [00:00 - 03:09]

So here we are, the end of the fat framework training. And I want to end it just to make sure that you've understood the key elements of what needs to be done, and you have implemented them. Now, some of you will have gone through the training just to see what it is or just to make sure you've got all the knowledge inside you before implementing. That's fine. I would really encourage you to make sure you've implemented every part of it or as much as you can, and then feel like you've completed it. Like this is not a case of you just get some education and you know, you're better for it. This is all about implementation. So by now, I would hope that you've implemented the three parts of the tracking Trinity. Number one is the browser pixel. Now, it's not a case of just having installed as you'll know, hopefully, if you've been to the training is to make sure it's installed as as optimally as it can be to get you the data quality that you need. The second part is the server pixel conversion API, Cappy, and making sure that's working as you need it to as well. And then the third part is the offline conversion. So with the three of these in place, then we can look at having better data in place. And as a reminder, this is kind of how we've set this all up. So the tracking Trinity is about taking as much quality data as you can and passing that back to the ad server. Then the next part of the checklist is making sure you have your Google Analytics UTM tracking in place. So if you've been through that part of the training, that should make sense. If not, then go back to there and make sure you've got your URL parameters in place for your ads. And by the way, it doesn't just have to be applied to Facebook. Those UTM tracking tags with a bit of modification can and should work for other platforms as well. Make sure you have your Google Analytics reporting in place as well. So the reporting is making sure you've got the free fat framework template for Google Analytics installed in your Google Analytics. Make sure you've configured it so it works and you can see your data and making sure that your naming conventions in your ad account are good enough so that you can see the data in Google Analytics and understand the data, understand the campaigns, the targeting and the ads that you're targeting as well. Then the next part is your Shopify reporting. Now I put this down as optional because not everyone that's been through this training will have Shopify and that's totally fine as well. If you do have Shopify, then make sure your data is coming through and you've been through that part of the training as well. And then really the final part is that my big outcome for you is that you're able to make better decisions with better data. So that's pushing more data into Facebook ads via the browser pixel, Cappy and offline conversions. So whatever's an ads manager is that much more reliable. And then the rest of it is using non Facebook ads manager analysis so that you can have a second and a third opinion on your data, Google Analytics and Shopify and obviously any other sales kind of platform you have where that data can be presented as well.

## [03:09 - 05:02]

So that with this, you're able to overcome a lot of the data limitations and you have the confidence to look at your numbers and make better decisions or make better decisions more frequently. I read a message from someone that said his strategy is just to launch lots of ad sets and ads if it works. Continue if it doesn't work, kill it. Problem is, what if the ad is working but you can't see the data and this is the biggest problem we have right now is in deciding where to place every dollar of your ad spend so that it's working in the best way. And you know, for a lot of you, you'll know this and you're implementing it, but it's not just about Facebook. It's also how is email performing, how is Google search performing, how are your YouTube ads performing, how is TikTok and anything else that you've got running. And that's really where the reporting the Google Analytics really comes into its own so you can see the data that's passing through those different channels and you can make better decisions as a result of it. So, you know, it might be the case that TikTok is converting amazingly well, but guess what? The top of funnel traffic is coming from Facebook or, you know, maybe your top of funnel is actually coming from YouTube, but it's converting through Facebook retargeting, which often happens as well. This is what it's about so that you can place more money in the top of funnel activities that are actually working and move away from last click attribution and as your kind of final decision point. So I'm confident that for most of you, hopefully all of you going through this training is going to be transformational. It has been for us. And as always, I'm just looking forward to seeing your success and, you know, best of luck with it. I want to see how you've implemented it, what that looks like. And I hope you guys have an amazing experience through this. I look forward to seeing your feedback.
